hints.fructus: 'fructus' is a noun meaning 'fruits' in the accusative plural
hints.dignos: 'dignos' is an adjective meaning 'worthy' in the accusative plural
hints.paenitentiae: 'paenitentiae' is a noun meaning 'of repentance' in the genitive

hints.Indicative: Indicative would state a fact — 'Facite' is a command
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive would express wish/possibility — 'Facite' is a direct imperative
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'facere' — 'Facite' is the 2nd person plural imperative

hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the parts of 'ferre' (to carry/bear)
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the parts of 'fugere' (to flee)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become) — the passive of 'facere'

hints.Nominative: 'fructus' could be nominative, but here it is the direct object of 'Facite' — accusative plural
hints.Genitive: Genitive singular is also 'fructus' (4th declension), but here it is accusative plural
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'fructu' — 'fructus' here is accusative plural

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'fructus' genitive ends in -ūs
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'fructus' genitive ends in -ūs
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'fructus' genitive ends in -ūs

hints.Nominative: Nominative singular is 'paenitentia' — 'paenitentiae' is genitive singular
hints.Dative: 'paenitentiae' could be dative, but here it means 'of repentance' — genitive modifying 'fructus'

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'paenitentiae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'paenitentiae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'paenitentiae' ends in -ae
